title: Leucanthemum pachyphyllum
intro: Leucanthemum pachyphyllum, commonly known as the daisy, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. Leucanthemum pachyphyllum contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support immune function, promote digestive health, and alleviate mild symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
taxonomy: Leucanthemum pachyphyllum is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is a member of the genus Leucanthemum, which comprises approximately 40 species of daisy-like plants. These plants are native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of North Africa. The classification of Leucanthemum pachyphyllum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Asteraceae family. As a member of the genus Leucanthemum, it shares similarities with other daisy-like plants in terms of their flower structure and growth habits.
